titleStmt.title: SII 33.30: original edition by K. Munirathnam and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 30. (A.R. No. 136 of 1946.) Nallacheruvupalle, Pulivendla Taluk, Cuddapah District.

summary.p: Slab set up near a well inside in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1453, Khara, Kārttika śu. 15, corresponding to 1531 A.D. October 25, Wednesday.

The inscription states that the king Achyutarāya gifted a land in the village Balapanūru as an agrahāra on Sarvamānya tenure to one Basavadīkshitulayya. Now certain measure of land in this agrahāra has been gifted to the god Bhairavēśvara of Mōpūru by Parivalūri Virusayya on the orders of his master Basavadīkshitulayya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu svasti śrī jayādbhu
daya śālivāhana śaka
va¨rṣaṁbulu 145
3 Agunēṭi kharasa
ṁvatsara kā¨rttika śu
(15) śrī mōpuri bayi
ravēśvaruniki (rēvō) dīkṣitu
la basavādīkṣitulayya
vāri Ānatini parivalūri vi
rusayyaṁgāru bayiravēśva
runiki dāravōśiyicci
na bhūdāna dha¨rmaśāsa
na kramameṭlaAnanu . ba
savādīkṣitulayyavā
rikini . śrī Acyutadē
varāya mahārāyalu
Agrahāraṁgā(na) pāliṁ
ccana vadhariṁccana balapanū
ri grāmamulōni yi śā
nyava 1 Acca . polu kala ba
ṁḍḍaku vuttarānanu yicci
na cenu 20 Akṣarālō
paṁdumu cēnuṁnu dāravō
siyistimi . Āpaṁdumu cē
ni palamuva lonu śrī ba
yiravēśvaruni dīpadūpanai
vēdyanakugānu dāravō
si yistimi Ācēnu sa

¨rvvamānyamugānu
Āceṁdrā¨rkasthāyi
gānu yistimi yi cē
nu jalaciṁna ja
la rāśamu nayasti
Ā cina tiruvēdhanā
yu śesu miru Aceni pa
lamu paraci dēvuni
dīpārādhana naivēdya
lines are damaged
kṣayala
puṁṇyamugā
nu dēvuni dīpārādhana
naivēdyaṁ naḍapukoni Ace
nuṁpuru Anabhaviṁcedi
yi dha¨rmunaku yavvaru va
kriṁccinānu vāri tallidaṁ
ḍru(lanu) kāśilōnu vadhiṁ
cina pāpānaboduru . gaṁga
(ka)ruta gobrāhmaṁnni vadhiṁci
na doṣānaboduru Anā
yiccina dha¨rmaśāsanamu
svadattāṁ dviguṇaṁ puṇyaṁ
paradattānupālanaṁ para
dattā pahāreṇa svada
ttāmu pālaṁ bhavēt
Digital edition of SII 33.30 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
